verses.text: David confronts corrupt judges: do they truly speak justice, or do they 'weigh the violence' of their hands? Power abuses its trust. Rulers who pervert justice answer to a higher Judge.
David confronts corrupt judges: do they truly speak justice, or do they 'weigh the violence' of their hands? Power abuses its trust. Rulers who pervert justice answer to a higher Judge.
The wicked are wayward from birth, 'like the deaf adder' that will not hear the charmer. Their hearts are closed to correction. Some so harden themselves that no appeal can reach them.
The wicked are wayward from birth, 'like the deaf adder' that will not hear the charmer. Their hearts are closed to correction. Some so harden themselves that no appeal can reach them.
The wicked are wayward from birth, 'like the deaf adder' that will not hear the charmer. Their hearts are closed to correction. Some so harden themselves that no appeal can reach them.
David prays God to break their fangs and make them melt away like water poured out. He asks God to disarm the cruel. The believer leaves the punishing of entrenched evil to God's hand.

David prays God to break their fangs and make them melt away like water poured out. He asks God to disarm the cruel. The believer leaves the punishing of entrenched evil to God's hand.

The righteous shall rejoice to see justice done, and men shall say, 'Verily there is a God that judgeth in the earth.' God's judgments vindicate His rule. However long wrong prevails, God will prove Himself the Judge of all.
